comment: This is generated by ESQL's FieldExtractTests. Do not edit it. See docs/reference/query-languages/esql/README.md for how to regenerate it.
name: field_extract
description: Extracts the value of a single sub-field from a `flattened` field root as `keyword`.

params.name: field
params.description: The root of a `flattened` mapping field. If `null`, the function returns `null`.

params.name: path
params.description: Literal name of the flattened sub-field to extract (e.g. `"host.name"`). Brackets and array indices are not supported. If `null`, the function returns `null`.

params.name: field
params.description: The root of a `flattened` mapping field. If `null`, the function returns `null`.

params.name: path
params.description: Literal name of the flattened sub-field to extract (e.g. `"host.name"`). Brackets and array indices are not supported. If `null`, the function returns `null`.

examples:
FROM flattened_otel_logs
| WHERE @timestamp == "2020-01-01T00:02:48.461Z"
| EVAL hn = field_extract(resource.attributes, "host.name")
| KEEP hn
